fields.description:
Capacity Scheduler queues have three modes:
 * relative/percentage
 * weight
 * absolute

Most of them have their own:
 * validation logic
 * config setting logic
 * effective capacity calculation logic

These logics can be easily extracted and encapsulated in separate config mode classes.

comments.body:
Uploaded an initial revision of this refactor. Currently I have skipped refactoring ManagedParent and AutoCreatedLeafQueue related logic. I have also kept this improvement as simple as possible. It is not justified yet to complicate things by:
 * Encapsulate child queue modes for a ParentQueue
 * Create new mode WEIGHT mode, as it is more of a subtype of RELATIVE mode

This might change, however, according to the needs of the tasks defined under this Jira eg. YARN-9936.

I have realised that minRatioPerResource was cached for a parent. I have incorporated the same for the config modes. Also found out, that minRatioPerResource was overwritten by each node label before, which is a bug. Current revision fixes these issues.

*Couple of comments:* 
 1. This is unused, can be removed: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.ParentQueue#getEffectiveMinRatioPerResource
 2. Also, the corresponding field is unused, never assigned: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.ParentQueue#effectiveMinRatioPerResource
 3. Nit: Unnecessary line break in: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#defineMode
 This can fit into one line:
{code:java}
    CapacityConfigType localType =
        CapacityConfigType.NONE;
{code}
4. What's the reason behind dropping the name "absolute" from class "ResourceConfigMode"? Wouldn't it be better as "AbsoluteResourceConfigMode"?

5. Just by reading the method signature of org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#getEffectiveMinRatioByResource, I have an idea for a follow-up jira:
 This is the method:
{code:java}
public Map<String, Map<String, Float>> getEffectiveMinRatioByResource() {
    return effectiveMinRatioByResource;
  }
{code}
As grouping any kind of numerical data (in this case, effective min. resource) by node labels is a common thing and it's scattered throughout the CS codebase, would you mind creating a follow-up jira to create an abstraction for it? As a start, it can be a simple class and we can evolve it later.
 If this is not clear, let's discuss it offline in detail.

6. Another follow-up candidate: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#calculateEffectiveRatio
 Can we split this up to some more easy to understand & reasonable, smaller methods?
 Also, a javadoc could be added to the method to explain what it is doing.

7. As this method a bit complex, would you mind adding a javadoc? 
 org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#validate

8. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#validate:

 It's also fine to do it in a follow-up as if you were modifying it with this jira, it would contradict with my general comment above :)

9. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#calculateEffectiveResource: 
 Same as in 8., the log statement could be a parameterized log instead of string concatenation.
 I do suggest a follow-up.

10. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#validate: 
 Same as in 8., the exception string statements are weird. I do suggest a follow-up.

11. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#calculateEffectiveResource and org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#deriveCapacityFromAbsoluteConfigurations: 
 These 2 methods are hard to read and complex. Would you mind creating a follow-up to split them or make them more readable?
 Also, a javadoc could be useful..

12. If you have the context, please add a javadoc to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#getMinResourceNormalized

comments.body:
Uploaded an initial revision of this refactor. Currently I have skipped refactoring ManagedParent and AutoCreatedLeafQueue related logic. I have also kept this improvement as simple as possible. It is not justified yet to complicate things by:
 * Encapsulate child queue modes for a ParentQueue
 * Create new mode WEIGHT mode, as it is more of a subtype of RELATIVE mode

This might change, however, according to the needs of the tasks defined under this Jira eg. YARN-9936.

I have realised that minRatioPerResource was cached for a parent. I have incorporated the same for the config modes. Also found out, that minRatioPerResource was overwritten by each node label before, which is a bug. Current revision fixes these issues.

Hi [~gandras],
 Thanks for working on this.
 *A general comment:*

This was a bit painful to review because of modifications + the reason that you moved huge code blocks over. For next time, I'd suggest to refactor in smaller steps, if possible.

 

*Couple of comments:* 
 1. This is unused, can be removed: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.ParentQueue#getEffectiveMinRatioPerResource
 2. Also, the corresponding field is unused, never assigned: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.ParentQueue#effectiveMinRatioPerResource
 3. Nit: Unnecessary line break in: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#defineMode
 This can fit into one line:
{code:java}
    CapacityConfigType localType =
        CapacityConfigType.NONE;
{code}
4. What's the reason behind dropping the name "absolute" from class "ResourceConfigMode"? Wouldn't it be better as "AbsoluteResourceConfigMode"?

5. Just by reading the method signature of org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#getEffectiveMinRatioByResource, I have an idea for a follow-up jira:
 This is the method:
{code:java}
public Map<String, Map<String, Float>> getEffectiveMinRatioByResource() {
    return effectiveMinRatioByResource;
  }
{code}
As grouping any kind of numerical data (in this case, effective min. resource) by node labels is a common thing and it's scattered throughout the CS codebase, would you mind creating a follow-up jira to create an abstraction for it? As a start, it can be a simple class and we can evolve it later.
 If this is not clear, let's discuss it offline in detail.

6. Another follow-up candidate: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.AbstractQueueConfigMode#calculateEffectiveRatio
 Can we split this up to some more easy to understand & reasonable, smaller methods?
 Also, a javadoc could be added to the method to explain what it is doing.

7. As this method a bit complex, would you mind adding a javadoc? 
 org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#validate

8. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#validate: 
 The exception texts are constructed in a weird way, like "Illegal" + " capacity sum". In general it could be more readable if String.format was used.
 It's also fine to do it in a follow-up as if you were modifying it with this jira, it would contradict with my general comment above :)

9. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.PercentageConfigMode#calculateEffectiveResource: 
 Same as in 8., the log statement could be a parameterized log instead of string concatenation.
 I do suggest a follow-up.

10. Nit: In org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#validate: 
 Same as in 8., the exception string statements are weird. I do suggest a follow-up.

11. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#calculateEffectiveResource and org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#deriveCapacityFromAbsoluteConfigurations: 
 These 2 methods are hard to read and complex. Would you mind creating a follow-up to split them or make them more readable?
 Also, a javadoc could be useful..

12. If you have the context, please add a javadoc to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.scheduler.capacity.queuemode.ResourceConfigMode#getMinResourceNormalized

*Generic comment to all the follow-ups (I counted 7 from my comments):*

 # Fixed
 # Fixed
 # Fixed
 # I have realised that we will need to introduce the RelativeResourceMode as well, which will inherit from ResourceMode. I think that a more generic naming is appropriate here.
